rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical difluoromethylation/Smiles rearrangement of N-arylsulfonyl acrylamides with CF2HSO2Na to afford β-difluoromethylamides via radical addition, aryl migration, and desulfonylation
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF2HSO2Na (sodium difluoromethanesulfinate) to generate CF2H radical with loss of SO2 and electron; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: CF2H radical generated from anodic oxidation of CF2HSO2Na
Intermediate A: tertiary C-radical from CF2H addition to acrylamide alkene
Intermediate B: spirocyclic cyclohexadienyl radical from ipso-attack on N-aryl ring (Smiles cyclization)
Intermediate C: N-centered (aminyl) radical after extrusion of SO2

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF2HSO2Na (Eox ~1.40 V) to release SO2 and CF2H radical, which adds to the acrylamide alkene of 1a forming carbon radical A; intramolecular ipso-attack on the N-aryl ring gives spirocyclic radical B, which loses SO2 to form nitrogen radical C; C is reduced/abstracts H (proton from trace water) to give product 3a while the cathode evolves H2.
rate_determining_step: Anodic oxidation of CF2HSO2Na to generate CF2H radical (irreversible oxidation peak at 1.40 V vs Ag/AgCl)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3g formed; TEMPO-CF2H adduct detected by HRMS (M+H+ 321.3189), confirming CF2H radical

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3g; BHT-CF2H adduct detected by HRMS (M+H+ 271.1848)

probe_reagents.name: D2O
probe_reagents.observation: In ultradry CH3CN with 10 equiv D2O, 43% D incorporation on N-H of product (39% yield), showing N-H hydrogen originates from water

probe_reagents.name: CD3CN/DCE (no D2O)
probe_reagents.observation: 0% D incorporation (34% yield), excluding solvent CH3CN as H source

probe_reagents.name: Divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a obtained in 65% yield in separated electrolytic cell, ruling out paired-electrolysis coupling requirement

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: CF2HSO2Na shows irreversible oxidation at 1.40 V; 1a oxidizes at 1.90 V (irreversible) — sulfinate is preferentially oxidized at the anode

notes: Smiles-type 1,4-aryl migration from sulfonyl-N to carbon with extrusion of SO2 is invoked. Control with divided cell shows pairing of anodic/cathodic half-reactions is not required for productivity. The N-H proton was shown to derive from adventitious/added water rather than acetonitrile solvent.
